What my Puppy Pampers look like with the gorgeous little star model @BaileyandFam 🥹 One thing I forgot to mention is that every Puppy Pamper is completely tailored to the puppy and built up gradually over their monthly sessions. The very first session is often just a bath, brush and blowdry to help them feel safe, relaxed and confident in the salon environment. For puppies that will eventually need haircuts, I’ll slowly introduce little bits such as trimming around the eyes, feet or hygiene areas, but only when I know they’re comfortable and happy with it 🥰 Sometimes it takes a few sessions of just bathing and drying before we move onto anything more. I’ll never push a puppy to do more than they’re ready for and my main goal with the sessions is to make the grooming process as positive and enjoyable for them as possible. We once discussed veterinary medicine from the perspective of how humans came to the idea of helping other living creatures while spending their own time and resources on it. Strictly speaking, this mostly applies to pets, because in the industrial world veterinarians often work with farm animals. Today I want you to think about how incredibly complex this science really is. A veterinarian has to understand the anatomy and physiology of dozens of different species in order to help them. From a tiny aquarium fish to a massive python, any creature may need care, and it is honestly impressive how veterinarians manage to keep all of this knowledge in their minds while making decisions that can determine the fate of their patients. Veterinarians save tens of thousands of beloved pets and prevent epidemics among farm animals, which in many ways also means protecting human populations. Thank you, veterinarians. #Veterinarian #Medicine #Animals #edit #Pets

This pizza came together in under 25 minutes and was delicious! Here is the recipe. Easy Summer Pizza Ingredients 	•	2 tbs olive oil 	•	1.5 cups cherry tomatoes 	•	1/4 tsp salt 	•	1/4 tsp pepper 	•	2 cloves garlic, grated 	•	1 tbs balsamic vinegar 	•	1 lb pizza dough 	•	Cornmeal, for rolling 	•	4 oz mozzarella pearls 	•	3 tbs romano cheese, grated 	•	Handful of fresh basil 	•	1 ball burrata 	•	Balsamic glaze Instructions In a skillet over medium heat, add the olive oil, cherry tomatoes, salt, and pepper. Cover and cook until the tomatoes begin to burst. Once softened, press them down with a spoon to mash. Stir in the garlic and cook for 1 minute more. Remove from heat and mix in the balsamic vinegar. Sprinkle cornmeal on your counter and roll out the pizza dough. Transfer to a baking sheet or pizza pan. Spread the tomato mixture over the dough, then top with mozzarella pearls and romano cheese. Bake at 450 for 15-18 minutes, or until the crust is golden and crisp. Top with fresh basil, tear open the burrata and dollop it around the pizza, then finish with balsamic glaze. Slice and enjoy!

yummy #junkfood #processedfood #sugar #slop ever have one of those days where you wake up thinking it’s going to be simple, calm, maybe even boring… and then the universe decides to roll out a full-on spectacle? Not a disaster. Not a tragedy. Just a slow, creeping chaos that nudges your plans off the table until all you can do is shrug and say, “Okay… let’s dance.” It’s like suddenly you’re the lead in a story you didn’t audition for. You were just trying to exist quietly, keep to your lane, maybe sip your coffee in peace. And then—bam—a whirlwind of people, events, and random coincidences crashes in. Everything ordinary turns loud, confusing, and somehow hilarious. There’s always that presence. The one that doesn’t leave, no matter how much you wish they would. Persistent, annoying, impossible to ignore. They challenge your patience, test your limits, make you laugh when you least expect it. And slowly, almost imperceptibly, they start to shift the world around you. Things feel lighter. Moments feel brighter. Walls you built begin to crumble. Then comes the unexpected twist. The one that doesn’t fit neatly into your plan, your comfort zone, your carefully curated life. They see past the surface—the tired, guarded exterior—and into the heart underneath. They don’t soften the chaos, they exist within it, and in doing so, force you to see yourself differently. Vulnerable, messy, real. It’s terrifying. It’s exhilarating. It’s alive. We all have our “safe corners,” spaces where the world feels predictable and quiet. But staying there too long? That’s not living. Growth waits in the interruptions, the detours, the moments that throw your day off balance. You don’t need to have a plan. You just need to keep showing up, keep opening doors, keep letting the story surprise you. Life isn’t linear. It’s a winding path of unexpected encounters, awkward coincidences, and loud, uninvited chaos. Some pass through and leave only echoes. Others stay long enough to shift the story, maybe even change it. And all of it—the frustration, the wonder, the laughter, the heartbreak—is yours. It shapes the narrative that only you can tell. So if today feels like a musical number you didn’t ask for, remember: you don’t need everything neat, perfect, or polished. You just need to move forward, to stumble, to dance a little, to let the interruptions in. Because in that chaos, in the mess, in the unplanned moments… that’s where you find the parts of yourself you didn’t know were waiting. And maybe, somewhere in all of it, you discover the story that’s truly yours—wild, imperfect, messy, and unforgettable. #targetaudience

This routine is for anyone who is not dealing with extreme damage but wants over all glossier & softer hair this is the the exact routine I built for getting glass hair on fine hair but will work for the wavy curly hair girls too!!  the science: the flatten your cuticle lies, the more light it reflects that's what gives the illusion of glass. glossy hair isn't a product, it's the consistency of routine over time that’s keeping that cuticle smooth from prewash to final styling  it took me a while to figure out the system, but once I did, the results compounded and now I wanna share my favourite tips with you because this is the routine that actually for every  wash day. the 5 things I never skip: 1. always create a barrier before shampoo: hair oil or conditioner first. less friction = less cuticle damage = more shine 2. weekly hair mask. deep hydration is what keeps that cuticle flat and the shine consistent. 3. a glossing treatment every 3-4 weeks  this is the step that takes glossy to glass  4. leave-in conditioner every single wash. beats frizz and keeps the cuticle flat between washes. 5. the hair sandwich method oil before heat styling AND after. then always finish with a cool shot to lock the cuticle in place ✨ Hope these haircare tips help you guys!! #hair #haircare #glasshair #hairgloss

#92i #makeup Le “92i makeup” fait référence à un style de maquillage inspiré de l’univers du rap français, surtout lié à Booba et à son label 92i (“92 International”, en référence au département des Hauts-de-Seine). Sur les réseaux sociaux, ce terme désigne un maquillage au style urbain et confiant : teint très net, contouring marqué, regard intense avec eyeliner ou faux cils, lèvres bien dessinées et une attitude sûre de soi. Ce n’est pas une technique officielle de maquillage, mais plutôt une esthétique inspirée du luxe, de la street culture et d’une image forte et assumée.
